local_issues.detail: Two ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT statements are unguarded (approval_instances_submitted_by_tenant_fkey, approval_signoffs_actor_tenant_fkey). On rerun they can fail with duplicate_object; wrap in DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS or DO $$ existence guards.

notes: Execution order is valid: 0134 approval_routes precedes 0135 approval_instances FK dependency; later grants/index migrations run after table creation.
Backward compatibility is preserved for Phases 1-4 via superset documents.status CHECK (0131), legacy remap (0132), and compat transition window in trigger (0133).
Trigger/event timing is coherent: transition and monotonic checks are BEFORE UPDATE; signoff controls are BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE; user_process_areas protections are BEFORE DELETE/UPDATE.
Security posture is strong: SECURITY DEFINER functions pin search_path to pg_catalog, pg_temp; role creation and app-role DDL are role-existence guarded; metaldocs schema USAGE grant is present.
All reviewed migrations use BEGIN/COMMIT and avoid transaction-incompatible DDL (no CONCURRENTLY operations).
